messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "frankfurt-cycle-lockout-year1",
 "title": "Buyer concentration × procurement timing empties the Year-1 window in the exact cell the ramp assumes",
 "narrative": "The 1%-of-SAM Year-1 benchmark implicitly assumes demand arrives smoothly from many independent buyers. But the venture's chosen cell — DE-heavy geography (0.50) times large operators (0.40) — is the lumpiest corner of the cube: a handful of Frankfurt/Amsterdam campus fit-outs whose electrical BoM, PDUs included, is specified at design and tender stage long before delivery. Capacity delivering in the venture's first 12 months was specced in 2024–25; capacity being specced now delivers after the window closes. The correlation between concentration and procurement timing means the truly contestable slice of SAM in months 0–12 is far below what a market-average benchmark implies — the YAM can miss even at the band's low end without a single node being 'wrong'.",
 "category": "execution",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Year-1 demand in the target cell is dominated by a few flagship fit-outs: Frankfurt alone is a 1.2GW market and Europe's 937MW of 2025 new supply is concentrated in a handful of campuses whose electrical procurement is locked at tender, well before the venture's launch window opens",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c3",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c6",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "With geo.DE=0.50 and cust.operator-large=0.40, the obtainable pool is not thousands of independent purchase decisions but a short list of lumpy, pre-committed projects — 85% of European take-up flows through FLAPD-scale operators whose specs are set upstream — so the smooth-arrival assumption behind the 1%-of-SAM benchmark fails precisely in this cell",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.DE",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c9"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "obtainableFactor 0.01 is a market-average Year-1 benchmark applied to a cell whose months-0–12 contestable share approaches zero; YAM is overstated for this specific geography×buyer mix even inside the stated 0.005–0.03 band",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "profile:Stage"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "obtainableFactor was already caught and corrected once (3%→1%) and now carries 'triangulated' maturity with a conservative-looking band — it reads as the most-scrubbed node in the funnel. The residual error is not the level but the correlation between buyer concentration and procurement timing, which no single-node sensitivity band can encode.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Two or more CE large-operator rack-PDU procurements in the venture's first 12 months that are open to unlisted vendors and run from RFQ to award to delivery inside that same window.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.55,
  "rationale": "Project lumpiness and upstream spec-lock in the target cell are directly documented in the pipeline data; the open question is only whether refresh/retrofit demand inside standing Frankfurt stock provides enough short-cycle volume to rescue the average.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.004,
   "note": "Year-1 contestable share conditioned on the DE×large-operator cell's spec-lock timing, below the generic band low"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Rack-PDU line items in EU-visible tenders with award-to-delivery under 12 months",
   "where": "TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) and operator supplier portals (Equinix/Digital Realty vendor registration → time to first RFQ)",
   "threshold": "Zero vendor-open short-cycle PDU tenders in CE over two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Ratio of CE capacity breaking ground (spec now, deliver later) vs delivering (specced already)",
   "where": "CBRE quarterly pipeline figures; Frankfurt/Amsterdam construction-start announcements on operator capex calls",
   "threshold": "Deliveries dominated by projects specced ≥18 months prior",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Audit the 2026–27 CE fit-out pipeline for spec-lock dates: which projects have not yet frozen their rack-power BoM and when do their tenders open",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Converts the benchmark into a pipeline-based obtainable model — the node's own promotion path"
  },
  {
   "action": "Re-weight Year-1 targeting toward brownfield refresh and retrofit in standing Frankfurt/Amsterdam stock, where PDU swap-outs transact inside 12 months without waiting for a new-build tender cycle",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Buys revenue in the only short-cycle sub-pool of the chosen cell"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "structure-independence",
 "riskId": "risk.structure-independence.frankfurt-cycle-lockout-year1"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

Capacity delivering in the venture's first 12 months was specced in 2024–25; capacity being specced now delivers after the window closes. Frankfurt/Amsterdam construction-start announcements on operator capex calls","threshold":"Deliveries dominated by projects specced ≥18 months prior","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Audit the 2026–27 CE fit-out pipeline for spec-lock dates: which projects have not yet frozen their rack-power BoM and when do their tenders open","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Converts the benchmark into a pipeline-based obtainable model — the node's own promotion path"},{"action":"Re-weight Year-1 targeting toward brownfield refresh and retrofit in standing Frankfurt/Amsterdam stock, where PDU swap-outs transact inside 12 months without waiting for a new-build tender cycle","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Buys revenue in the only short-cycle sub-pool of the chosen cell"}]}
